Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 1, 064 [Namburbu][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

NAM.BUR₂.BI ḪUL UR.GI₇ ša₂ ina E₂ LU₂ i-bak-ku-u i-⸢dam⸣-[mu-mu]

(o 1) Apotropaic ritual for dispelling the evil of a dog which cries (and) howls in the house of a man, or has sprinkled a man with its urine, and then the evil of [...] dog [...] not [...] the man and his house.

o 22

lu KAŠ₃-MEŠ-šu₂ LU₂ is-lu-⸢ḫu šup⸣-šu₂-rim-ma ḪUL UR.GI₇ [...]

o 33

a-na LU₂ u₃ E₂-šu₂ NU [x x]

o 44

DU₃.DU₃-BI ⸢UR⸣.GI₇ ša₂ IM ⸢DU₃⸣uš gišere-nu ina GU₂ GAR-šu₂ I₃.GIŠ ana ⸢SAG⸣.[DU-šu₂ x x]

(o 4) Its ritual: You make a dog from clay. You put cedar-wood on it at the neck. [...] oil on its head.

o 55

SIG₂ UD₅ MU₄.MU₄-su zap-pi ANŠE.KUR.RA ina KU.⸢UN⸣-šu₂? GAR [...]

(o 5) You wrap it in goat's hair. You put a tuft of horse's hair on its tail. You set up an offering table [...]. You set out 12 loaves (made) from emmer. [...] dates (and) saskû-flour.

o 66

GI.DU₈ GUBan 12 NINDA IMGAGA tara-kas₂ ZU₂.LUM.MA ⸢zi₃EŠA⸣ [x x]

o 77

NINDA.DE₂.AM₃ LAL₃ I₃.NUN.NA GARan dugA.DA.⸢GUR₅ GUB?⸣an

(o 7) You put down a cake (made) from honey (and) butter. You set up a libations vessel.

o 88

2 dugNIG₂.TA?.KUR₄-MEŠ KAŠ SAG SA₅-ma GUBan NIG₂.NA ⸢šim⸣LI [x x]

(o 8) You fill 2 kurkurru-vessels with first-class beer and set (them) up. [...] a censer (full) of juniper.

o 99

KAŠ SAG BALqi₂ NA BI tu-šak-mas-su-ma NU UR.GI₇ ša₂ ⸢IM⸣

(o 9) You libate the first-class beer. You make that man kneel and he raises the image of that dog (made) from clay and you have him speak as follows.

o 1010

šu-a-te IL₂-ma ki-a-am tu-šad-babab-šu₂

o 1111

EN₂ dUTU LUGAL ANe u KIti₃ DI.KU₅ AN.TA-MEŠ u KI.TA-MEŠ

(o 11) Incantation: Šamaš, king of heaven and earth, judge of the upper world and the lower world!

o 1212

ZALAG₂ DINGIR-MEŠ mur-te-du-u LU₂ti da-i-ni de-e-nu ša₂ DINGIR GAL-MEŠ

(o 12) Light of the gods, guide to humanity, judger of legal decisions for the great gods!

o 1313

as-ḫur-ka aš₂-ʾe-ka ina DINGIR-MEŠ qi-bi TI.⸢LA⸣

(o 13) I have searched for you, I have sought you. Among the gods decree life for me.

o 1414

DINGIR-MEŠ ša₂ KI-ka liq-bu-u MUNUS.SIG₅ti₃ aš₂-šu₂ UR.GI₇ ⸢ŠEŠ⸣e

(o 14) May the gods who are with you decree prosperity for me. Because of this dog which urinated its urine over me, I am frightened, and I am afraid, and I am constantly terrified.

o 1515

ša₂ KAŠ₃-MEŠ-šu₂ ana UGU-ia iš-ti-nu pal-ḫa-[ku-ma]

o 1616

ad-ra-ku u₂ šu-ta-du-ra-⸢ku⸣ ḪUL UR.⸢GI₇⸣ [...]

(o 16) Make the evil of [...] dog pass me by and ... your praises.

o 1717

šu-ti-qa-an-ni-ma da₃-li₂-li₂-⸢ka⸣ [...]

o 1818

⸢ki-ma⸣ an-nam-ma 3-šu₂ IGI dUTU DU₁₁.DU₁₁ ana UGU [...]

(o 18) When he has said this 3 times before Šamaš, [...] on top [...].

o 1919

[...] x ⸢ana di-na-ni⸣-[ia x x]

(o 19) [...] .... [...] as my substitute.

o 2020

⸢mim-ma ḪUL?⸣ [...]

(o 20) [...] everything evil [...].

o 2121

aš₂-ḫu-ṭu₂ aš₂-⸢ḫu⸣-[ṭu₂ x] x [...]

(o 21) I have stripped off, I have stripped off [...] ... [...].

o 2222

aš₂-ḫu-ṭu₂ mim-ma lem-nu ša₂ ⸢UZU?⸣-[MEŠ-ia₅ ... mim]-⸢ma⸣ lem-nu ⸢ša₂⸣ la?-[ni?-ia₅? ...]

(o 22) I have stripped off [...] everything evil in my flesh, [...] everything evil in my outward form.

o 2323

[aš₂]-⸢ḫu-ṭu₂⸣ mim-ma lem-nu ša₂ IGI-⸢ia₂⸣ u ⸢EGIR⸣-[ia₂] ⸢ana⸣ UGU-⸢ka⸣

(o 23) I have stripped off on top of you (the dog's image) everything evil that is before me and behind me.

o 2424

[...] DU₁₁.GAu₂ TA IGI [d]⸢UTU⸣ DU₈-⸢ma⸣

(o 24) [...] you have said [...], you move away from the presence of Šamaš and [...] ... he speaks as follows.

o 2525

[...] x UR₅.GIM ⸢DU₁₁.GA⸣

o 2626

[... ka]-⸢la?⸣-a-ma ana-ku? NENNI ⸢IBILA⸣ [x]

(o 26) [...] everything, I, so-and-so, the son of [...], [...] sprinkled me [...] and I am frightened and [...], [...] cannot [...] to its place, the evil of the dog [...] ..., [...] of that dog, ... let me always pray to you.

o 2727

[... is-lu]-⸢ḫa⸣-an-ni-ma ⸢pal⸣-ḫa-⸢ku-ma⸣

o 2828

[...] ⸢ana KI⸣-šu₂ la₃ [x x] ḪUL UR.GI₇

o 2929

[...] x [x UR].GI₇ UR₅tu₂

o 3030

[... lu-uk-tar]-⸢rab⸣-ka

o 3131

[...] lud-lul

(o 31) [...] let me sing [...].

o 3232

[...] x la tu-[ma-aš-ša-ri-šu₂ ...]

(o 32) [...] .... You may not release it (the dog). [...].

(rest of obverse missing)
Reverse
rr NaN  (start of reverse missing)
r 1'r 1'

[x] x x x x pal-ḫa-ku ad-[ra-ku šu]-⸢ta⸣-du-ra-ku

(r 1') [...] ..., I am frightened, I am afraid, I am constantly terrified.

r 2'2'

[i]-⸢na⸣ ḪUL UR.GI₇ ŠEŠe šu-ti-⸢qa⸣-[an-ni]-ma

(r 2') Make (afflictions) pass me by from the evil of this dog and let me sing your praises.

r 3'3'

⸢da₃⸣-li₂-li₂-ka lud-⸢lul⸣

r 4'4'

[x] ⸢ḪUL⸣ GISKIM UR.GI₇ BUR₂ IŠ x [x x] AŠ₂

(r 4') [...] releasing the evil in the sign of a dog ... [...] ....

r 5'5'

NU UR.GI₇ ša₂ IM DU₃uš IGI x [x x] ⸢KUN⸣-šu₂ ŠID

(r 5') You make an image of the dog from clay. Before ... [...] you recite [...] its tail.

r 6'6'

ana qid-da-at ID₂ ⸢SU₃ ḪUL⸣ BI

(r 6') He plunges into the downstream of the watercourse. That evil will [...] approach the man (and) his house.

r 7'7'

ana ⸢NA⸣ E₂-šu₂ [x] TEe

r 8'8'

⸢EN₂⸣ den-ki lugal ⸢abzu-ke₄?⸣ [x eridugki]-⸢ga⸣ ḫe₂-me-⸢en⸣

(r 8') Incantation: Enki, you are the king of Abzu, the [...] of Eridug.

r 9'9'

ŋeškim ḫul ur-gir₁₅ igi-⸢zu-še₃⸣ ba-⸢gilim?⸣

(r 9') The evil sign of the dog lies across in front of you.

r 10'10'

ba-ra-an-teŋ₃-ŋe₂₆-e-de₃ ba-ra-an-gi₄-⸢gi₄⸣-[de₃]

(r 10') May it not approach him, may it not turn to him.

(colophon)
r 11'11'

GIMma LIBIR.RA?-⸢šu₂⸣ SAR-ma ba-ri₃

(r 11') Written according to its original and then checked.

r 12'12'

ša₃-ṭir₂ mmu-šal-lim—dba-ba₆ ŠAB.TUR a-ga-aš-[gu-u]

(r 12') The writer is Mušallim-Baba, the scribal apprentice (and) novice, the son of Qurdi-Nergal, šangû-priest of Zababa and Baba of the centre of Arbaʾil (and) of the centre of Harran and Huzirina.

r 13'13'

⸢A?⸣ mqur-di—dNERGALₓ(U.GUR) lu₂SANGA dza-ba₄-ba₄

r 14'14'

u dba-ba₆ ša₂ qe₂-reb iriLIMMU₂?.DINGIR-MEŠ

r 15'15'

ša₂ qe₂-reb iriKASKAL u iriḫu-ziri-⸢na⸣
